# FinPilot AI — API Response → UI Mapping
> For Member 3 (Integration). Every JSON field mapped to exact UI element, component, and behaviour.

---

## HOW TO READ THIS DOC

Each section follows this pattern:
```
API endpoint → request shape → response shape → UI element that consumes it
```

State management note: use a simple React context or Zustand store.
Each feature has its own slice: `taxState`, `fireState`, `healthState`, `xrayState`.

---

## GLOBAL — Shared across all screens

### Disclaimer
Every API response includes:
```json
{
  "disclaimer": "This analysis is generated by an AI...",
  "is_licensed_advice": false
}
```
| Field | UI Element | Component | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|---|
| `disclaimer` | Footer disclaimer banner | `<DisclaimerBanner>` | Always visible at bottom of every screen |
| `is_licensed_advice` | Amber pill in top bar | `<StatusPill variant="ai-generated">` | Always shows "Not financial advice" |

---

## SCREEN 1 — Tax Regime Optimizer

### API Endpoint
```
POST /api/tax/calculate
```

### Request (sent on "Calculate Tax" button click)
```json
{
  "salary": 1800000,
  "hra_received": 360000,
  "rent_paid": 30000,
  "city_type": "metro",
  "deduction_80c": 150000,
  "deduction_80d": 25000,
  "nps_80ccd": 50000
}
```

### Form field → request field map
| UI Input Label | Field Name | Validation | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Annual Salary (₹) | `salary` | required, > 0 | Raw number, no commas |
| HRA Received (₹) | `hra_received` | optional, default 0 | |
| Rent Paid/month (₹) | `rent_paid` | optional, default 0 | Store as monthly, API uses monthly |
| City Type | `city_type` | "metro" or "non-metro" | Dropdown value |
| 80C Deductions (₹) | `deduction_80c` | max 150000 | Show warning if user enters > 1.5L |
| 80D Health Insurance (₹) | `deduction_80d` | max 25000 | |
| NPS Contribution (₹) | `nps_80ccd` | max 50000 | |

### Response shape
```json
{
  "old_regime": {
    "taxable_income": 1274500,
    "deductions": 525500,
    "tax_before_cess": 213350,
    "cess": 8534,
    "total_tax": 221884
  },
  "new_regime": {
    "taxable_income": 1725000,
    "deductions": 75000,
    "tax_before_cess": 257500,
    "cess": 10300,
    "total_tax": 267800
  },
  "winner": "old",
  "savings": 45916,
  "reasoning": "The old regime is significantly better for you because...",
  "disclaimer": "...",
  "is_licensed_advice": false
}
```

### Response field → UI element map

**Old Regime Card** (`response.old_regime`):
| JSON Field | UI Element | Format | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `old_regime.total_tax` | Large bold number, card header | `₹2,21,884` | toLocaleString('en-IN') |
| `old_regime.taxable_income` | Table row "Taxable Income" | `₹12,74,500` | |
| `old_regime.deductions` | Table row "Total Deductions" | `₹5,25,500` | |
| `old_regime.tax_before_cess` | Table row "Tax" | `₹2,13,350` | |
| `old_regime.cess` | Table row "Cess (4%)" | `₹8,534` | |
| `old_regime.total_tax` | Table row "Final Tax" | `₹2,21,884` | bold |

**New Regime Card** (`response.new_regime`): same structure as above.

**Winner logic**:
| JSON Field | UI Element | Condition | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|---|
| `winner` | Card border color | `winner === "old"` | Old card gets `border: 2px solid #10B981` |
| `winner` | "Best for you" badge | always on winner card | `<StatusPill variant="best">` |
| `savings` | Green savings banner | always shown | `"You save ₹45,916 by choosing old regime"` |

**AI Explanation Card** (`response.reasoning`):
| JSON Field | UI Element | Component | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|---|
| `reasoning` | Body text | `<AIExplanationCard>` | Render as plain text, no markdown parsing needed |

### Loading & error states
| State | Trigger | UI |
|---|---|---|
| Loading | Button clicked, awaiting response | Skeleton shimmer on both result cards |
| Error | Network fail or 500 | Red inline error message below button: "Something went wrong. Try again." |
| Empty | Before first calculate | Hide results section entirely (`display: none`) |

---

## SCREEN 2 — FIRE Planner

### API Endpoint
```
POST /api/fire/calculate
```

### Request
```json
{
  "current_age": 34,
  "retire_age": 50,
  "annual_income": 2400000,
  "monthly_expense": 80000,
  "current_savings": 1500000,
  "expected_return": 0.12,
  "inflation": 0.06
}
```

### Form field → request field map
| UI Input Label | Field Name | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Current Age | `current_age` | integer | number input |
| Target Retire Age | `retire_age` | integer | slider, range 40–65 |
| Annual Income (₹) | `annual_income` | float | |
| Monthly Expenses (₹) | `monthly_expense` | float | |
| Current Savings (₹) | `current_savings` | float | default 0 |
| Expected Return | `expected_return` | float | slider 8–15%, divide by 100 before sending |
| Inflation (hidden) | `inflation` | float | hardcode 0.06, don't show to user |

### Response shape
```json
{
  "corpus_needed": 24000000,
  "sip_per_month": 38400,
  "years_to_retire": 16,
  "feasibility": "stretch goal",
  "annual_expense_at_retire": 960000,
  "chart_data": [
    {"age": 34, "projected": 1500000, "required": 1800000},
    {"age": 35, "projected": 2180000, "required": 2300000},
    ...
    {"age": 50, "projected": 18000000, "required": 24000000}
  ],
  "reasoning": "Your FIRE plan is ambitious but achievable...",
  "disclaimer": "...",
  "is_licensed_advice": false
}
```

### Response field → UI element map

**Metric Cards** (top row):
| JSON Field | UI Component | Format | Card Label |
|---|---|---|---|
| `corpus_needed` | `<MetricCard>` | `₹2.4 Cr` (divide by 10M, 1 decimal) | "Corpus Needed" |
| `sip_per_month` | `<MetricCard>` | `₹38,400` | "SIP Per Month" |
| `years_to_retire` | `<MetricCard>` | `16 yrs` | "Years to Retire" |

**Format helper for crores:**
```js
const formatCr = (n) => n >= 10000000 
  ? `₹${(n/10000000).toFixed(1)} Cr` 
  : `₹${(n/100000).toFixed(1)} L`
```

**Feasibility Pill** (`response.feasibility`):
| Value | Pill Variant | Color | Icon |
|---|---|---|---|
| `"on track"` | success | #10B981 green | ✓ |
| `"stretch goal"` | warning | #F59E0B amber | ⚠ |
| `"needs revision"` | danger | #EF4444 red | ✗ |

**Line Chart** (`response.chart_data`):
| Field | Chart Role | Color | Style |
|---|---|---|---|
| `chart_data[].age` | X axis | — | Label every 2 years |
| `chart_data[].projected` | Line 1 "Your corpus" | #6366F1 indigo | Solid line |
| `chart_data[].required` | Line 2 "Required corpus" | #EF4444 red | Dashed line |

Use Recharts `<LineChart>`. Tooltip shows both values at hovered age point.

**What-if recalculation:**
Same endpoint, same request — just update `retire_age` from the inline input and re-POST.
Show only the 3 metric cards updating; don't re-render the full chart (jarring UX).

**AI card**: `response.reasoning` → `<AIExplanationCard>`

---

## SCREEN 3 — Money Health Score

### Two-phase flow: Onboarding → Score

### Phase 1: Onboarding (no API call until all 6 steps done)

Store answers locally as user progresses:
```js
const healthInput = {
  emergency_months: "3-6",     // Step 1 option selected
  has_term_insurance: true,    // Step 2
  has_health_insurance: true,  // Step 2
  monthly_sip: 15000,          // Step 3 number input
  total_investments: 500000,   // Step 3
  has_home_loan: false,        // Step 4
  emi_to_income_ratio: 0.15,   // Step 4 (auto-calc: EMI/income)
  tax_regime_optimized: false, // Step 5 boolean
  annual_income: 1200000,      // Step 6
  epf_nps_contribution: 50000  // Step 6
}
```

**Step → field map:**
| Step | Question shown | Field stored | Input type |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 Emergency | "Months of expenses covered?" | `emergency_months` | 4 option buttons: "<1" / "1-3" / "3-6" / ">6" |
| 2 Insurance | "Do you have term insurance?" | `has_term_insurance` | Yes/No |
| 2 Insurance | "Do you have health insurance?" | `has_health_insurance` | Yes/No |
| 3 Investments | "Monthly SIP amount (₹)" | `monthly_sip` | number input |
| 3 Investments | "Total investment value (₹)" | `total_investments` | number input |
| 4 Debt | "Do you have a home/car loan?" | `has_home_loan` | Yes/No |
| 4 Debt | "Monthly EMI (₹)" | `monthly_emi` | number input (show if has_home_loan = true) |
| 5 Tax | "Have you chosen tax regime this year?" | `tax_regime_optimized` | Yes/No |
| 6 Retirement | "Annual income (₹)" | `annual_income` | number input |
| 6 Retirement | "EPF + NPS contribution/year (₹)" | `epf_nps_contribution` | number input |

### API call (after step 6, on "See my score" button)
```
POST /api/health/score
```
Send entire `healthInput` object.

### Response shape
```json
{
  "overall_score": 72,
  "dimensions": {
    "emergency": 85,
    "insurance": 60,
    "investments": 78,
    "debt": 90,
    "tax_efficiency": 45,
    "retirement": 38
  },
  "grade": "Good",
  "priority_actions": [
    {
      "dimension": "retirement",
      "score": 38,
      "severity": "high",
      "title": "Boost retirement savings",
      "description": "You're at 38/100. Start NPS or increase EPF contribution.",
      "cta": "Fix this"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "tax_efficiency",
      "score": 45,
      "severity": "medium",
      "title": "Optimize tax efficiency",
      "description": "Claim NPS 80CCD(1B) benefit — save up to ₹15,600.",
      "cta": "Fix this"
    }
  ],
  "reasoning": "Your overall financial health is good...",
  "disclaimer": "...",
  "is_licensed_advice": false
}
```

### Response → UI map

**Circular Score Gauge** (`response.overall_score`):
| Field | Element | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `overall_score` | Large number inside circle | 40px bold |
| `overall_score` | Arc fill % | `score/100 * 360` degrees |
| `grade` | Badge below gauge | "Good" → amber, "Excellent" → green, "Needs Work" → red |

**Score color logic:**
```js
const scoreColor = (s) => s >= 70 ? '#10B981' : s >= 40 ? '#F59E0B' : '#EF4444'
```

**Dimension Cards** (`response.dimensions`):
| JSON Key | Card Label | Progress bar fill | Score display |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dimensions.emergency` | "Emergency Fund" | scoreColor(value) | right-aligned |
| `dimensions.insurance` | "Insurance Coverage" | scoreColor(value) | right-aligned |
| `dimensions.investments` | "Investments" | scoreColor(value) | right-aligned |
| `dimensions.debt` | "Debt Health" | scoreColor(value) | right-aligned |
| `dimensions.tax_efficiency` | "Tax Efficiency" | scoreColor(value) | right-aligned |
| `dimensions.retirement` | "Retirement Readiness" | scoreColor(value) | right-aligned |

**Priority Action Cards** (`response.priority_actions[]`):
| Field | UI Element | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `severity === "high"` | Left border color | `#EF4444` red |
| `severity === "medium"` | Left border color | `#F59E0B` amber |
| `title` | Card heading | 15px bold |
| `description` | Card body text | 13px muted |
| `cta` | Link button | "Fix this →" — on click, navigate to relevant tab |

**CTA navigation:**
```js
const ctaRoutes = {
  retirement: '/fire-planner',
  tax_efficiency: '/tax-optimizer',
  emergency: '/fire-planner',
  insurance: null,  // show external advisory note
  investments: '/portfolio-xray',
  debt: null        // show advisory note
}
```

---

## SCREEN 4 — MF Portfolio X-Ray

### API Endpoint
```
POST /api/portfolio/analyze
```

### Request (built from fund entry table)
```json
{
  "funds": [
    {
      "name": "Mirae Asset Large Cap",
      "amount_invested": 100000,
      "current_value": 142000,
      "category": "large_cap"
    },
    {
      "name": "Parag Parikh Flexi Cap",
      "amount_invested": 80000,
      "current_value": 115000,
      "category": "flexi_cap"
    },
    {
      "name": "Axis Midcap Fund",
      "amount_invested": 60000,
      "current_value": 78000,
      "category": "mid_cap"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Fund table → request map:**
| Table Column | JSON Field | Validation |
|---|---|---|
| Fund Name | `funds[].name` | required, non-empty string |
| Amount Invested (₹) | `funds[].amount_invested` | required, > 0 |
| Current Value (₹) | `funds[].current_value` | required, > 0 |
| Category dropdown | `funds[].category` | one of: large_cap / mid_cap / flexi_cap / elss / debt / international |

Minimum 2 funds required — show inline validation error if only 1 entered.

### Response shape
```json
{
  "total_invested": 240000,
  "total_current": 335000,
  "xirr": 14.2,
  "benchmark_return": 12.1,
  "avg_expense_ratio": 1.4,
  "overlap_matrix": {
    "Mirae Asset Large Cap": {
      "Parag Parikh Flexi Cap": 42,
      "Axis Midcap Fund": 8
    },
    "Parag Parikh Flexi Cap": {
      "Axis Midcap Fund": 15
    }
  },
  "allocation": {
    "large_cap": 42,
    "flexi_cap": 34,
    "mid_cap": 24
  },
  "overlap_severity": "high",
  "reasoning": "Your portfolio has significant overlap between...",
  "disclaimer": "...",
  "is_licensed_advice": false
}
```

### Response → UI map

**Metric Cards** (top row of 4):
| JSON Field | Label | Format | Color logic |
|---|---|---|---|
| `xirr` | "Portfolio XIRR" | `14.2%` | green if > benchmark, red if < |
| `avg_expense_ratio` | "Avg Expense Ratio" | `1.4%` | green < 1%, amber 1–1.5%, red > 1.5% |
| `xirr - benchmark_return` | "vs Nifty 50" | `+2.1%` | green if positive, red if negative |
| `overlap_severity` | "Overlap" | Pill: Low/Medium/High | green/amber/red |

**Overlap matrix** (`response.overlap_matrix`):
```js
// Build symmetric matrix from the nested object
// overlap_matrix[fundA][fundB] = overlap %
// Color: < 20 → green cell, 20-40 → amber cell, > 40 → red cell
const cellColor = (pct) => 
  pct < 20 ? '#DCFCE7' : pct < 40 ? '#FEF9C3' : '#FEE2E2'
```
| Element | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Row/column headers | Fund names (shortened to 15 chars) | `name.substring(0, 15)` |
| Cell value | `overlap_matrix[a][b]` | Show as `42%` |
| Cell bg color | `cellColor(value)` | |
| Diagonal cells | n/a | Show `—` (same fund) |

**Allocation bar** (`response.allocation`):
Each key in `allocation` = one segment. Width = percentage value.
```js
const allocationColors = {
  large_cap: '#6366F1',
  mid_cap: '#10B981',
  flexi_cap: '#F59E0B',
  elss: '#3B82F6',
  debt: '#8B5CF6',
  international: '#EC4899'
}
```

**AI card**: `response.reasoning` → `<AIExplanationCard>`

---

## REUSABLE COMPONENT PROPS REFERENCE

### `<AIExplanationCard>`
```tsx
<AIExplanationCard
  text={response.reasoning}       // string
  isLoading={isLoading}           // boolean → shows skeleton
  isCollapsed={false}             // boolean → collapsed view
/>
```

### `<MetricCard>`
```tsx
<MetricCard
  label="Corpus Needed"
  value="₹2.4 Cr"
  variant="default" | "positive" | "negative"
  isLoading={isLoading}
/>
```

### `<StatusPill>`
```tsx
<StatusPill
  variant="success" | "warning" | "danger" | "best" | "ai-generated"
  label="On Track"
/>
```

### `<DisclaimerBanner>`
```tsx
<DisclaimerBanner
  text={response.disclaimer}
  variant="inline" | "sticky-footer"
/>
```

### `<ActionCard>`
```tsx
<ActionCard
  severity="high" | "medium"     // controls left border color
  title="Boost retirement savings"
  description="You're at 38/100..."
  ctaLabel="Fix this"
  onCtaClick={() => navigate('/fire-planner')}
/>
```

---

## ERROR HANDLING — ALL SCREENS

Standard error response from any endpoint:
```json
{
  "error": true,
  "code": "INVALID_INPUT",
  "message": "Annual salary must be greater than 0"
}
```

| Error code | UI behaviour |
|---|---|
| `INVALID_INPUT` | Show inline field error below the relevant input |
| `CALCULATION_ERROR` | Show error card in results area: "We couldn't complete this calculation. Please check your inputs." |
| `NETWORK_ERROR` | Toast notification top-right: "Connection issue. Please try again." |
| `CLAUDE_UNAVAILABLE` | Show results without AI explanation card. Show note: "AI insights unavailable right now — showing numbers only." |

---

## LOADING STATE CHECKLIST

For every screen, implement these loading states:

```js
const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle') 
// idle → loading → success → error

// idle: show form only, no results
// loading: disable button, show skeleton on result area
// success: show full results
// error: show error message, re-enable button
```

Button loading state:
```jsx
<button disabled={status === 'loading'}>
  {status === 'loading' ? 'Calculating...' : 'Calculate Tax'}
</button>
```

---

## ROUTING STRUCTURE (React / Next.js)

```
/ → redirects to /tax
/tax → Tax Optimizer screen
/fire → FIRE Planner screen
/health → Money Health Score screen
/portfolio → Portfolio X-Ray screen
```

Active tab in sidebar = current route. Use `usePathname()` to detect.

Bottom mobile tab bar uses same routes.

---

## ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES NEEDED

```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000   # local dev
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=https://your-railway-app.railway.app  # production
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...   # server-side only, never expose to client
```

All API calls go through your own backend (`/api/*`).
Never call Anthropic directly from the frontend.
